prompt: I see both `spyglass.spikesorting.v0` and `spyglass.spikesorting.v1` imports in old lab code. Which should I use for a fresh session today and why?

behavioral_checks: Recommends v1 for new work; identifies v0 as legacy and directs the user not to populate v0 tables for new sessions
Routes to spikesorting_v0_legacy.md only for querying pre-v1 sortings, not for new work
Does not recommend v0 for new sessions or as the default — v0 is for reading legacy data only

expected_output: Routes to spikesorting_v1_pipeline.md (v1 flow) and spikesorting_v0_legacy.md (read-only access to pre-v1 sortings). Recommends v1 for all new work; identifies v0 as legacy — do not populate v0 tables for new sessions, and query existing v0 sortings through the merge wrapper `SpikeSortingOutput.CuratedSpikeSorting` rather than the v0 tables directly. Names `SpikeSortingOutput` as the merge table surfacing both v0 (CuratedSpikeSorting part) and v1 (`SpikeSortingOutput.CurationV1` part) sortings uniformly for downstream consumers.
